title: श्री वासुदेवाष्टकम्
devanagariText:
श्रीवासुदेवं परमं पदं ये
वदन्ति विष्णुं प्रभवं जगत्ताम्।
तमादिदेवं प्रणतोऽस्मि नित्यं
वासुदेवं शरणं प्रपद्ये॥१॥

वसुदेवगृहे जातं देवकीनन्दनं विभुम्।
नन्दगोपप्रियं बालं वासुदेवं नमाम्यहम्॥२॥

मथुरापुरनिर्माणं कंसभीतं जगत्पतिम्।
गोकुले लालितं श्रीशं वासुदेवं नमाम्यहम्॥३॥

गोपीजनप्रियं कृष्णं राधामाधवमव्ययम्।
वृन्दावनविहारिणं वासुदेवं नमाम्यहम्॥४॥

कालियदमनं वीरं गोवर्धनधराव्ययम्।
गोपालं गोकुलानन्दं वासुदेवं नमाम्यहम्॥५॥

मथुरानाथमीशानं कंसमर्दनकारिणम्।
उग्रसेनप्रतिष्ठातुं वासुदेवं नमाम्यहम्॥६॥

द्वारकाधीशमीशानं रुक्मिणीवल्लभं प्रभुम्।
षोडशस्त्रीसहस्रेशं वासुदेवं नमाम्यहम्॥७॥

पार्थसख्यं कृपासिन्धुं गीताज्ञानप्रबोधकम्।
धर्मसंस्थापकं देवं वासुदेवं नमाम्यहम्॥८॥

वासुदेवाष्टकं पुण्यं यः पठेत् प्रयतः सदा।
वासुदेवप्रसादेन सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री वासुदेवाष्टकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
Shrivasudevam Paramam Padam Ye
Vadanti Vishnum Prabhavam Jagattam.
Tam Adidevam Pranato'smi Nityam
Vasudevam Sharanam Prapadye.||1||

Vasudeva-Grihe Jatam Devaki-Nandanam Vibhum.
Nandagopa-Priyam Balam Vasudevam Namamyaham.||2||

Mathura-Pura-Nirmanam Kamsa-Bhitam Jagatpatim.
Gokule Lalitam Shrisham Vasudevam Namamyaham.||3||

Gopi-Jana-Priyam Krishnam Radha-Madhavam Avyayam.
Vrindavana-Viharinam Vasudevam Namamyaham.||4||

Kaliya-Damanam Viram Govardhana-Dharavyayam.
Gopalam Gokula-Anandam Vasudevam Namamyaham.||5||

Mathura-Natham Ishanam Kamsa-Mardana-Karinam.
Ugrasena-Pratishtha-tum Vasudevam Namamyaham.||6||

Dvarakaadhisham Ishanam Rukmini-Vallabham Prabhum.
ShodashaStri-Sahasresham Vasudevam Namamyaham.||7||

Partha-Sakhyam Kripaasindhum Gitajnanaprabodhakam.
Dharmasamsthaa-pakam Devam Vasudevam Namamyaham.||8||

Vasudeva-Ashtakam Punyam Yah Pathet Prayatah Sada.
Vasudeva-Prasadena Sarvasiddhim Avapnuyat.

|| Iti Shri Vasudeva Ashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
श्री वासुदेव को जो परम पद कहते हैं, जो विष्णु जगत के प्रभव हैं — उन आदिदेव को मैं नित्य प्रणत हूँ, वासुदेव की शरण लेता हूँ।

वसुदेव के गृह में जन्मे, देवकीनन्दन, विभु, नन्दगोप के प्रिय बालक वासुदेव को मैं नमन करता हूँ।

मथुरापुरी में अवतरित, कंस से भयभीत जगत के पति, गोकुल में लालित श्रीश वासुदेव को नमन।

गोपीजनप्रिय कृष्ण, राधामाधव, अव्यय, वृन्दावनविहारी वासुदेव को नमन।

कालिय दमन करने वाले वीर, गोवर्धन धारी, गोपाल, गोकुलानन्द वासुदेव को नमन।

मथुरानाथ, कंस-मर्दन करने वाले, उग्रसेन को राज्य पुनः स्थापित करने वाले वासुदेव को नमन।

द्वारकाधीश, रुक्मिणीवल्लभ, सोलह हजार स्त्रियों के ईश प्रभु वासुदेव को नमन।

पार्थ (अर्जुन) के सखा, कृपासिन्धु, गीताज्ञान प्रबोधक, धर्मसंस्थापक देव वासुदेव को नमन।

जो सदा पवित्र भाव से वासुदेवाष्टक पढ़ता है, वह वासुदेव प्रसाद से सर्वसिद्धि प्राप्त करता है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीवासुदेवाष्टकमन्त्रस्य नारद ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप्-इन्द्रवज्रा छन्दः। श्रीवासुदेव देवता। ॐ नमो भगवते वासुदेवाय बीजम्। सर्वसिद्धिशक्तिः। वासुदेवप्रसादः कीलकम्। श्रीवासुदेवप्रीत्यर्थे पाठे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Covers the complete journey of Vasudeva Krishna from birth to Mahabharata
Invokes sarva siddhi (all accomplishments) through vasudeva's grace
Each verse corresponds to a major phase of Krishna's divine play
Ideal for daily devotion and Janmashtami celebration
Combines bhakti with the essence of Krishna's life story
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and leads to the supreme abode
description: Dedicated to Lord Krishna, the Vasudeva Ashtakam (श्री वासुदेवाष्टकम्) is an eight-verse devotional hymn revered in Hindu tradition. Rooted in Traditional Vaishnavite Stotra, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to combine bhakti with the essence of Krishna's life story as well as destroy all sins and leads to the supreme abode. Additionally, it is known to grant Sarva Siddhi (all accomplishments) through Vasudeva's grace. This prayer is traditionally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Janmashtami and Gita Jayanti to invoke divine grace. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 5 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Vasudeva Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vasudeva Ashtakam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight verses covering Krishna's complete life journey for devotion and all-round success.
relatedSearches: Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya mantra benefits
Krishna life story Bhagavata Purana summary
Janmashtami stotras to chant for Krishna birth
Krishna Dwarka leela Rukmini sixteen thousand

seoFaqs.question: Who is Vasudeva and how is the name used in this Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: Vasudeva carries two related meanings in this hymn. First, it is a patronymic name of Krishna, son of Vasudeva Anakadundubhi of the Yadava clan, which is the literal sense in verse 2. Second, it is a theological title meaning the All-Pervading Lord (vasati = dwells, sarvasmin = in all), one of the four Pancharatra vyuhas (Vasudeva, Sankarshana, Pradyumna, Aniruddha). The Ashtakam uses the name in both senses, addressing Krishna as the historical son of Vasudeva and as the cosmic Vasudeva-tattva, the supreme Brahman who dwells in all beings. The Bhagavata declares vasudevah sarvam-iti, all is Vasudeva.

seoFaqs.question: What is the structure of the Vasudeva Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ashtakam follows Krishna's life chronologically across its eight verses: verse 1 establishes Vasudeva as the supreme Brahman; verse 2 narrates his birth in Vasudeva's house and his transfer to Nandagopa; verse 3 describes his Mathura origin and Gokul upbringing; verse 4 his Vrindavan-leela with the gopis and Radha; verse 5 his heroic Kaliya-mardana and Govardhan-dharana; verse 6 his return to Mathura and slaying of Kamsa; verse 7 his Dwarka kingship with Rukmini and the 16,008 queens; verse 8 his role as Arjuna's friend and the speaker of the Bhagavad Gita. This life-summary structure makes the Ashtakam ideal for Janmashtami katha and Krishna-leela meditation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the source tradition of the Vasudeva Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ashtakam belongs to the traditional Vaishnava stotra corpus and shares its life-narrative structure with several Krishna-Ashtakams from the Bhagavata Purana commentary tradition and the Pancharatra Agama liturgy. The viniyoga preserved in the text identifies Narada as the rishi, Anushtup and Indravajra as the chhandas, Vasudeva as the devata, and Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya as the bija mantra. The 12-syllable Dwadashakshari Vasudeva mantra is the most fundamental Vaishnava mantra after Om itself and is described in the Bhagavata, the Vishnu Purana and the Pancharatra Samhitas.

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya in this hymn?
seoFaqs.answer: The viniyoga identifies Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya, the 12-syllable Dwadashakshari mantra, as the bija of the Ashtakam. This mantra is held to be the supreme Vaishnava mantra capable of liberating its reciter without any other sadhana. The Bhagavata Purana narrates that Sage Narada gave it to the boy Dhruva, who through its japa attained the supreme abode and became the Pole Star. It is the same mantra recited 24-times daily by every devotee of the Pancharatra tradition. By being grounded in this mantra, the Vasudeva Ashtakam carries the same liberative potency.

seoFaqs.question: When should the Vasudeva Ashtakam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Wednesday (Budhvar), Krishna's day, is the primary weekly day for recitation. Janmashtami (Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami) and Gita Jayanti (Margashirsha Shukla Ekadashi) are the principal annual occasions. Ekadashi is particularly auspicious as it is sacred to Vishnu. Daily recitation by Vaishnava devotees forms part of nitya-karma. There are no caste, gender or initiation restrictions; the hymn is open to all. Tradition prescribes recitation while seated facing east or north, before an image of Krishna or Vasudeva-Krishna with offerings of tulasi leaves, butter and milk-based sweets.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Vasudeva Ashtakam phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti states that whoever recites the punya Vasudeva Ashtakam with purity attains Sarva Siddhi, all accomplishments, by the grace of Vasudeva. The viniyoga additionally identifies the kilaka as Vasudeva-prasada (Krishna's grace itself), implying that the hymn's potency comes not from ritual mechanism but from Krishna's spontaneous favour. Devotees recite it for protection across Krishna's eight life-phases (birth-troubles, child-protection, gopi-bhakti, Kaliya/Govardhan-style enemy removal, Kamsa-style liberation from oppression, Dwarka-style prosperity, Arjuna-style guidance and Gita-jnana). The cumulative effect is held to grant both worldly success and final moksha to Vaikuntha.
